title: Lore
text:
I have been interested in AI for a long time, especially since I saw the AlphaGo Documentary and read the book "Life 3.0“, which I read EOY 2021. 

Demis Hassabis strategy of "Solve intelligence and then use it to solve everything else." resonated with me.

I was convinced AGI could be created within my lifetime and I wanted to contribute by dedicating my life to researching and working on it. 

I believed that intelligence was entirely physical and therefore reproducible in machines. I also believed that it was not impossibly hard and therefore achievable.

But AGI still remained abstract for me.

I was very impressed with early LLMs like GPT 3 and later GPT 4, and I was the first one to show GPT 3 in the OpenAI Playground to my English teacher in my last year of school.

The moment I was completely and utterly convinced of their significance was December 20th 2024, upon seeing the ARG AGI score of o3 on the OpenAI livestream.

I believed that the moment AI would achieve economic utility to the point where the research can fund itself would be the start of a kind of self-improvement, where more money would result in more research and therefore better AI which would generate more money, eventually resulting in superhuman systems.

I believed that even if the reasoning paradigm would always lack something fundamental to the generality of human intelligence, models would at some point become such competent coders and researchers that they could meaningfully contribute to figuring out the paradigm that would result in actual AGI.

But  it is now clear that even without AGI, AI agents will completely reshape the economy to the point where I am no longer employable.
